title: 2,4-Dihydroxybenzophenone benzoic hydrazone for spectrophotometric determination of copper(II) and iron(III) in soil and cement
author: V. Krishna Reddy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Sri Krishnadevaraya University, Anantapur-515 003, India 

  Manuscript received 23 May 1997, revised 28 December 1998, accepted 4 February 1999  

 2,4-Dihydroxybenzophenone benzoic hydrazone ( DHBPBH)  is employed as an analytical reagent for the spectrophotometric determination of copper and iron. The reagent forms 1 : 1 greenish yellow coloured complex with copper(II) at pH 4.0 and λ max  380 nm (E 1.55 x 10 4  dm 3  mol -1  cm -1 ); Sandell's sensitivity 0.004 µg cm -2 , and Beer's law range 0.31-2.2 µg ml -1 . It results a brown coloured 2: 3 complex with iron(lll) at pH 5.0 and λ max  380 nm (E 2.8 x 10 4  dm 3  mol -1  cm -1 ); Sandell's sensitivity 0.002 µg em -1  and Beer's law range 0.14-1.0 µg ml -1 . The method has been applied for the determination of copper in soil and alloy samples and iron( Ill) in cement sample.
